tests.scenario: Free vs paid feature list shown before onboarding
steps: Launch the app as a new user for the first time
Observe the first screen presented
tests.expected_result: A screen listing free and premium features is displayed before any personal questions or account creation prompts appear.

tests.scenario: Feature list is visible without scrolling past onboarding questions
steps: Launch the app as a new user
Attempt to bypass the feature list screen and go directly to onboarding questions
tests.expected_result: The system does not allow the user to start onboarding questions without first viewing the free vs paid feature summary.

tests.scenario: Basic assessment summary available after 14-day free period without subscription
steps: Complete 14 days of check-in responses as a free-tier user
Navigate to the assessment results section
tests.expected_result: A basic summary of mood trends and a general recommendation is displayed without any subscription prompt or paywall.

tests.scenario: Subscription upsell does not block access to basic results
steps: Complete 14 days of check-in responses as a free-tier user
Tap on the results/report section
Observe whether a paywall or subscription prompt blocks the content
tests.expected_result: At minimum a basic mood trend summary is shown. A subscription upsell may be presented as an option but must not be the only content displayed.

tests.scenario: Daily check-in completes within two minutes
steps: Open the daily check-in screen
Start a timer
Complete all check-in questions
Submit the response and stop the timer
tests.expected_result: The entire check-in flow takes less than two minutes from opening to submission confirmation.

tests.scenario: Check-in response is saved to mood history immediately
steps: Complete and submit one check-in session
Navigate to the mood history section
tests.expected_result: The just-completed check-in entry appears in the mood history list with the correct timestamp and scores.

tests.scenario: Mood history dashboard displays 7-day view
steps: Log check-ins for at least 7 consecutive days
Navigate to the mood history dashboard
Select the 7-day time range
tests.expected_result: A chart or graph is displayed showing aggregated mood scores for the past 7 days, with one data point per day.

tests.scenario: Mood history dashboard supports 30-day and 90-day views
steps: Navigate to the mood history dashboard
Switch between the 30-day and 90-day time range options
tests.expected_result: The dashboard updates to show the correct data range for each selected period. All available check-in data within the range is reflected in the chart.

tests.scenario: User can configure three reminders at custom times
steps: Open app settings and navigate to the reminders section
Enable reminders and set three different custom times (e.g., 8:00, 13:00, 20:00)
Save the settings
tests.expected_result: The settings are saved and the system schedules notifications for all three specified times. The settings screen confirms the three reminder times are active.

tests.scenario: Push notification arrives within five minutes of scheduled time
steps: Set a reminder time for two minutes from the current time
Lock the phone screen and wait
tests.expected_result: A push notification from MindDoc arrives within five minutes of the configured time.

tests.scenario: Notification delivered after app has been closed for over one hour
steps: Set a reminder time for 90 minutes from now
Close the app completely (force stop)
Wait for the scheduled notification time
tests.expected_result: The push notification is delivered at the scheduled time even though the app was closed for more than one hour.

tests.scenario: Battery optimization guidance prompt is shown on first reminder setup
steps: Set up the first reminder on a new installation
Observe whether a battery optimization guidance prompt appears
tests.expected_result: The system displays a one-time prompt explaining how to exclude MindDoc from battery optimization, with a direct link or button to the device's battery settings.

tests.scenario: User can access check-in in anonymous mode without account creation
steps: Launch the app as a new user
Select the anonymous or guest mode option
Attempt to complete a daily check-in
tests.expected_result: The check-in screen opens and the user can complete and submit a check-in without having created an account or provided any personal information.

tests.scenario: Guest mode data is stored locally and not transmitted
steps: Enable guest mode and complete several check-ins
Disable the device's internet connection
Navigate to the mood history section
tests.expected_result: Mood history from guest mode is fully accessible without an internet connection, confirming data is stored locally on the device.

tests.scenario: App lock activates after one minute of background inactivity
steps: Enable app lock with a PIN in settings
Switch to another app and wait two minutes
Return to MindDoc
tests.expected_result: The PIN entry screen is displayed and no personal mood or journal data is visible until the correct PIN is entered.

tests.scenario: App lock persists after force close and relaunch
steps: Enable PIN lock and configure a PIN
Force close the app
Relaunch the app
tests.expected_result: The PIN entry screen is displayed immediately upon relaunch and no content is visible until authentication succeeds.

tests.scenario: Five therapeutic exercise modules available in free tier
steps: Log in as a free-tier user
Navigate to the exercises or programs section
tests.expected_result: At least five complete exercise modules are listed and accessible without a subscription prompt. Each module is fully interactive.

tests.scenario: Each free exercise module includes description, steps, and completion confirmation
steps: Open any free-tier exercise module
Complete all steps of the exercise
tests.expected_result: The module displays: a title and description, step-by-step instructions, and a completion confirmation screen or checkmark after all steps are finished.

tests.scenario: Educational info card displayed when recurring mood pattern is detected
steps: Log consistently low morning mood scores for seven consecutive days
Navigate to the mood summary or insights screen
tests.expected_result: At least one educational information card relevant to the detected pattern (e.g., low morning mood) is displayed, with a plain-language explanation referencing a psychological framework.

tests.scenario: Educational card references a recognized psychological framework
steps: Trigger an educational card by logging a recurring pattern
Read the content of the displayed information card
tests.expected_result: The card explicitly mentions or is linked to a recognized framework (e.g., CBT, mindfulness-based stress reduction) and does not contain generic wellness advice only.

tests.scenario: PDF export contains mood chart and check-in data
steps: Log at least 10 check-in responses across multiple days
Navigate to the mood history screen
Initiate a PDF export
tests.expected_result: A PDF is generated containing a mood trend chart and a table of check-in scores with dates. The PDF is downloadable or shareable from the device.

tests.scenario: PDF is shareable via native device share menu
steps: Generate a PDF export
Tap the share button
tests.expected_result: The device's native share menu opens, allowing the user to send the PDF via email, messaging apps, or cloud storage.

tests.scenario: Plain-language privacy summary displayed on first launch
steps: Uninstall and reinstall the app
Launch the app for the first time
tests.expected_result: A privacy consent screen is displayed showing a summary (not only a link) that lists data types collected, third-party sharing practices, and data deletion steps.

tests.scenario: User must acknowledge privacy summary before proceeding
steps: Launch the app for the first time
Attempt to skip the privacy summary without acknowledging it
tests.expected_result: The app does not allow the user to proceed to onboarding or any other screen until they have explicitly acknowledged the privacy summary (e.g., by tapping an Accept button).
